name: Cefotaxime
executiveSummary: Third-generation cephalosporin. Treatment of lower respiratory tract, skin and soft tissue, bone, joint, intra-abdominal, genitourinary, and central nervous system infections caused by susceptible gram-negative bacteria.
indications:
## Main indications

Third-generation cephalosporin. Treatment of lower respiratory tract, skin and soft tissue, bone, joint, intra-abdominal, genitourinary, and central nervous system infections caused by susceptible gram-negative bacteria.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

pediatrico.reconstitution: Reconstitute with SWFI. Dilute in 0.9% saline or 5% dextrose.
pediatrico.finalConcentration: 20 to 60 mg/mL (may be bolus 100 mg/mL over 3 to 5 min).
pediatrico.dose: < 12 years: 100-150 mg/kg/day every 6-8 h. > 12 years and adults: 1-2 g every 6-8 h, maximum dose: 12 g. Meningitis: 300 mg/kg/day every 6 h. Cystic fibrosis: 150-200 mg/kg/day every 6 h, maximum dose: 12 g. Adult preoperative dose: 1 g.
pediatrico.notes: Caution in patients with renal insufficiency. Increased risk of pseudomembranous colitis due to C. difficile.

neonatal.dose: 50 mg/kg/dose every 12 h; meningitis: every 8 h (NICU).

stability:
## Pediatric guide

- 24 h at room temperature and 10 days refrigerated (2 to 8°C).
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Neutropenia, hemolytic anemia, headache, pseudomembranous colitis, phlebitis at injection site, rash, anal pruritus.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — cefOTAxime (code 0037, ATC J01DD).
Institutional pediatric dilution and administration guide. June 2026.
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
Argentine Society of Infectious Diseases (SADI). Guidelines and consensus statements.
